summary: Executed backend-only regression checks for Android native release-linker failure mitigation. Verified templates/workflow now enforce lld and disable IPO/LTO controls for JNI/native targets; all tests passed.
action_items: Run GitHub Actions Android build once to confirm real runner link command no longer includes gold/LLVMgold.so dependency and release arm64-v8a completes.
If CI still emits -flto=thin from transitive toolchain flags, add explicit release c/cpp/link flags to disable LTO at project level in generated Android build.
critical_code_review_comments: No critical template defects found in referenced files for requested linker/LTO safeguards.
Coverage here is static/template validation; runtime CI environment behavior still requires live workflow verification.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Added three focused regression tests: (1) app-build.gradle enforces -DANDROID_LD=lld and -DCMAKE_INTERPROCEDURAL_OPTIMIZATION=OFF across default/debug/release; (2) cmake-android-jni-append.cmake sets target IPO FALSE and target_link_options -fuse-ld=lld; (3) build-android.yml appends the JNI cmake template via sed substitution into generated CMakeLists.
rca of the issue: From failure logs, release native link used -fuse-ld=gold with thin LTO, requiring LLVMgold plugin unavailable on runner and causing ELF machine/plugin errors. Current templates mitigate by forcing lld and turning off INTERPROCEDURAL_OPTIMIZATION at config and target levels.
